title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Behavior Case: Ship Facing
summary: Thrust teleports position, diagonal motion is faster, velocity aliases facing, turning changes old momentum, drag depends on frames, and speed caps each component independently. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ship facing increment in frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.
Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Ship Facing, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_ship_motion_physics_5(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-ship-facing-iun321
# Competency: asteroids-ship-facing.
# Case 2193: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_ship_facing_iun321(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iun321 = "iun321-123nui-2193"
    assert evidence_variant_iun321
    evidence_axes_iun321 = (
        "playeriun321",
        "seed2193",
        "input123nui",
        "frameiun3212193",
        "state2193iun321",
        "playtest123nui2193",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iun321)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Ship Facing: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6076 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing, keep Ship Facing connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Ship Facing precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6076 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Ship Facing model · Theory Ship Facing, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: The ship must always move exactly where it faces.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-ship-facing-iun321(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_ship_facing_iun321\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(speed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?thrust \* dt)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?1.0 - drag \* dt)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(damped, max_speed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
